---
title: "Gallery"
description: "Analyses and outbreak responses built with the EpiAware packages."
toc: false
page-layout: full
---

Analyses and outbreak responses built with the EpiAware packages.
The Julia packages are young, so this is a short list for now, and it will grow.
Used the tools yourself? [Add your work](#add-your-work).

For the wider reading and the R tools this work grows out of, see [Resources](resources.qmd).

::: {.gallery-grid}

::: {.gallery-card}
::: {.gallery-banner .g-teal}
Ebola delay estimation
:::
::: {.gallery-body}
### BDBV — delay distributions and stratified CFR
Bayesian estimation of delay distributions and the case-fatality ratio from the 2012 Isiro Bundibugyo Ebola line list, using the composed-distributions approach.

[View analysis ↗](https://epiforecasts.io/bdbv-linelist-analysis/dev/)
:::
:::

::: {.gallery-card}
::: {.gallery-banner .g-deep}
Andes virus modelling
:::
::: {.gallery-body}
### ANDV — joint incubation, transmission, and growth
Joint estimation of incubation periods, transmission timing, and the reproduction number for Andes virus (hantavirus), combining delay and renewal modelling.

[View analysis ↗](https://epiforecasts.io/andv-linelist-analysis/dev/)
:::
:::

::: {.gallery-card}
::: {.gallery-banner .g-amber}
Outbreak size
:::
::: {.gallery-body}
### Estimating current outbreak size
Real-time estimation of the size of the 2026 DRC Bundibugyo ebolavirus outbreak, demonstrating the kind of rapid-response analysis the tools are designed for.

[View analysis ↗](https://epiforecasts.io/BVDOutbreakSize/stable/)
:::
:::

:::

## Add your work {#add-your-work}

Used EpiAware, CensoredDistributions.jl, or another package for an analysis or model?
Share it on the [epinowcast forum](https://community.epinowcast.org/) or open a pull request adding a card here.
A short description, a link, and it goes a long way.
